item.title: review architect agent always gets Edit/Write permissions regardless of --update-docs flag
item.intent: cmd_review without --update-docs gives architect Read,Glob,Grep only; --update-docs gives Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write.
item.description: In run_agent(), the architect case checks if output_file is non-empty to decide permissions. Since cmd_review always sets output_file (for output capture), the architect always gets PERMS_REVIEWER_DOCS (Edit,Write), even when --update-docs is not specified. The fix: resolve permissions in cmd_review based on the update_docs flag rather than relying on output_file as a proxy. Without --update-docs the review should be truly read-only.
steps: In cmd_review, after parsing --update-docs, set a local variable for the required permissions: PERMS_REVIEWER if read-only, PERMS_REVIEWER_DOCS if update-docs
Pass the resolved permissions to run_agent, or temporarily override CFG_PERMS_REVIEWER before the run_agent call
In run_agent architect case, remove the output_file-based permission check — it was the wrong proxy
Verify: run .aishore/aishore review and confirm --allowedTools does not include Edit or Write
Verify: run .aishore/aishore review --update-docs and confirm --allowedTools includes Edit,Write
acceptanceCriteria: cmd_review without --update-docs invokes architect with Read,Glob,Grep permissions only (no Edit or Write)
cmd_review --update-docs invokes architect with Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write permissions
Existing behavior for other agent roles (developer, validator, tech-lead, product-owner) is unchanged
